title: [RFE]: Add support for `buildx bake` command
body:
Docker supports higher-level builds using the [`buildx bake`](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/buildx_bake/) command. The [command introduction page](https://docs.docker.com/build/bake/) provides a good overview about the use-cases enabled by this command:

> Buildx also aims to provide support for high-level build concepts that go beyond invoking a single build command. We want to support building all the images in your application together and let the users define project specific reusable build flows that can then be easily invoked by anyone.
>
> BuildKit efficiently handles multiple concurrent build requests and de-duplicating work. The build commands can be combined with general-purpose command runners (for example, `make`). However, these tools generally invoke builds in sequence and therefore cannot leverage the full potential of BuildKit parallelization, or combine BuildKit’s output for the user. For this use case, we have added a command called `docker buildx bake`.

Personally, I'm mostly interested in this command because it allows getting rid of various hackish scripts and `make` files and replaces them with a single command: `buildah builx bake`.

That being said, the command is still experimental in Docker and has a pretty big API surface (lots of flags and config options) so it might not be trivial to support it in buildah. But I think even a simplified version of this command that only covers the basic stuff could still provide a lot of value to buildah/podman users.

comments.body: Sure interested in opening some PRs to start building it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Can't resist a joke. But since Docker does not support this, does that mean it is half baked?  :^)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
How does one specify what you want to bake?  Multiple Containerfiles?
@flouthoc PTAL
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@umohnani8 We might want to look at something like this after podman farm build.

Wouldn't it make sense to point buildkit to the podman socket at some point?  I fear it's not that sustainable to chase buildkit which is evolving very rapidly.  Similar to `podman compose`, a hypothetical `podman buildx` would call buildkit but point it to the podman socket.

That would allow to focus on the REST API.
watching this.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Great feature, we have a pipeline to build 20+ targets, it will help
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Also find this one valuable. We have functionality relying on `docker buildx Bake` and we'd like to move to Buildah and Podman but have been hesitant due to this lack of functionality.

Same use-case here, we use `bake` to build multiple targets that share the same image context concurrently, makes the pipeline command `docker buildx bake` rather than hundreds of lines of multiple docker builds and build args.

Docker Desktop is crashing on my Mac, checking through the logs it has something to do with volumes not being attached or whatever... The problem is that I heavily rely on buildx. As a workaround solution, you can have both podman and Docker Desktop installed. Docker should not run. In podman desktop go to Settings and copy podman socket endpoint. Then you can create a new context in Docker ```bash docker context create --docker host=unix:///var/folders/pc/45t66f792252lszjxx4bch280000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock podman ``` Then switch the docker's default context ```bash docker context use podman ``` This way you can keep using `docker buildx`, but everything will happen inside of podman. NOTE: I am not sure whether podman docker compatibility plugin has to be enabled.
FWIW, I've started working on a small tool to build Bake files using Buildah: https://github.com/emersion/bakah

I'd love support for `docker buildx bake` like functionality from buildah/podman.

I can't make the switch before podman/buildah supports it.

It is highly advantageous to use `docker buildx bake` to generate `bakefile.hcl` or `bakefile.json` for large-scale hierarchical image building.

It solves the problem of batch building multiple dockefiles in the warehouse. Through some simple logical arrangements, a bakefile.json or a bakefile.hcl written in the project can be used to declare the build parameters, dependency context, and other information for each project.

However, I encountered some problems when using it, and this issue has also been noticed by other projects in the community. For example:

Docker Bake uses BuildKit and by default it pushes attestation. Its format does not follow the OCI specification, so it is not supported by the OCI registry. The common practice in the community is to build the image locally and then use docker push to push it.


Our mirror repository was initially built using buildah in GitHub Actions. However, it became difficult to handle the dependencies of the image context and manage numerous image arrangements. Later, we switched to docker bake.

It is still hoped that the community can have similar tools available, even if they do not follow the front-end design of Docker Bake, but they should be able to solve this kind of problem.
any updates? I love the declare style like hcl to build container image
Is this still active? I can pick this up
Then I would also vote for this issue. This would help me a lot in the work context as we need to build quite some dependent images in-house
